name: Fine Margins: Fast Goals and Tight Wins at World Cup 2026
parameter: World Cup 2026 second round of group games
angle: Friday's second-round group games were settled in the margins: Ismael Saibari struck after 70 seconds to win the fastest goal of the tournament as Morocco beat Scotland, Matias Galarza's early strike sank Turkiye, the USA clinched a knockout place without the injured Pulisic, Brazil came alive under Ancelotti, and Haiti became the first team out.

questions.question: The United States clinched a knockout-round place by beating Australia on Friday. What was the score?
Brazil's 3-0 win sent which nation home as the first team eliminated from World Cup 2026?
Morocco beat Scotland in Boston to take control of their group. What was the final score?
Who scored after just 70 seconds — one of the quickest goals of the 2026 World Cup — to settle the Scotland game?
Brazil's two-goal hero against Haiti scored twice before Vinicius Junior added the third. Who was he?
The USA beat Australia despite missing their captain and talisman, ruled out by injury. Who sat it out?
Paraguay stunned Turkiye 1-0 with an early strike. Who scored the winner?
Friday's Brazil v Haiti and Scotland v Morocco fixtures were both played in the same group. Which one?
The USA's second goal against Australia was finished by a young defender after a VAR offside reversal. Who was it?
Scotland were aggrieved when a late penalty appeal was waved away. Which midfielder went down in the box?
Saibari's strike against Scotland made him the first Morocco player to achieve what?

options.A: Captain Morocco at a World Cup
options.B: Score in three different World Cups

questions.question: Paraguay held on to beat Turkiye with ten men after a first-half red card to which forward?
Who supplied the assist for Saibari's winner, lifting the ball over the Scotland defence?
Which Turkiye goalkeeper was beaten by Galarza's early goal as his side dominated possession but lost?
Brazil's win came under Carlo Ancelotti, the first foreign coach to lead them at a World Cup. As a manager, how many times has he won the Champions League — a record?

sources: https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/6/20/morocco-beat-scotland-1-0-as-saibari-scores-fastest-world-cup-2026 (Al Jazeera — Saibari's 70-second goal, fastest of WC2026; first Morocco player to score in consecutive World Cups)
https://theanalyst.com/articles/scotland-vs-morocco-stats-fifa-world-cup-2026-live (Opta — Brahim Diaz assist; McTominay penalty appeal turned down)
https://www.skysports.com/football/united-states-of-america-vs-australia/report/549797 (Sky — USA 2-0 Australia, Cameron Burgess own goal + Alex Freeman via VAR; USA clinch knockout spot)
https://www.npr.org/2026/06/19/nx-s1-5863602/2026-fifa-world-cup-usmnt-australia (NPR — USA top Australia 2-0 without injured Pulisic)

https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49119519/brazil-haiti-live-world-cup-2026-latest-updates-commentary-score-result (ESPN — Brazil 3-0 Haiti: Matheus Cunha brace, Vinicius Junior; Ancelotti's side; Haiti first team eliminated)
https://www.foxsports.com/watch/fmc-ce2628rxscvzc91p (FOX — Matias Galarza early goal past Ugurcan Cakir; Almiron red card; Turkiye 32 shots)

https://www.cbssports.com/soccer/news/carlo-ancelotti-to-brazil-italian-manager-signs-with-brazil-on-reported-one-year-deal-through-2026-world-cup (CBS — Ancelotti, first foreign coach to lead Brazil at a WC; record five Champions League titles as a manager)
